frame_analyses.description: Initial setup frame showing peg board with 6 red rubber objects positioned on left side pegs. No instruments yet visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS peg transfer setup with bilateral graspers ready.

frame_analyses.description: Left-hand grasper (appearing to be non-dominant) approaching first peg on left side. Grasper opening to grasp rubber object.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good approach angle, instrument positioned for secure grasp.

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper holding first red object in mid-air, beginning transfer toward right-side peg. Right grasper visible in frame.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth object control, stable grasp maintained during transfer.

frame_analyses.description: Right-hand grasper positioning to receive/place the transferred object onto right-side peg. Object being guided into position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled placement, right grasper assisting in positioning object on peg.

frame_analyses.description: Second object being grasped from left side peg. Left grasper repositioning for next pickup.
Multiple objects in various stages of transfer. Red loop visible connecting transferred objects on right side pegs.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be mid-sequence with several objects successfully transferred. Smooth hand coordination.

frame_analyses.description: Objects accumulating on right-side pegs. Bilateral grasper coordination visible as more objects are placed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good peg placement, objects stacking properly on right side pegs.

frame_analyses.description: Transition phase beginning - right grasper now picking up from right side pegs to initiate reverse transfer. Left grasper repositioning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear transition to reverse phase. Objects now being transferred back to left side.

frame_analyses.description: Multiple objects in mid-air transfer from right back to left side pegs. Red loop visible throughout transfers indicating consistent object control.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth reverse sequence execution. Good mid-air control of transferred objects.

frame_analyses.description: Objects being repositioned back onto left-side pegs. Final placements being made on original side.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Methodical placement of remaining objects back to start position.

frame_analyses.description: Nearly all objects transferred back to left side. Red loop clearly visible showing final object positions.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task nearing completion, all 6 objects being repositioned back to original locations.

frame_analyses.description: Final object placement. Last red rubber object being positioned on left-side peg. Graspers preparing to release.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean final placement, task completion imminent.

penalties.description: All 6 rubber objects successfully transferred and repositioned. No objects dropped outside field or lost.
All objects properly placed on pegs throughout task. No objects left floating or improperly positioned.
Task completed well before 300-second maximum time limit. All 6 objects successfully repositioned.

task_specific_assessments.hand_coordination: excellent - smooth bilateral coordination between graspers
task_specific_assessments.transfer_smoothness: excellent - fluid transitions between pickup, midair transfer, and placement

confidence_rationale: Task completion clearly visible across all 12 frames. Completion time precisely estimable from frame sequence. All objects successfully transferred with no drops or placement errors observed. Bilateral grasper visibility and control excellent throughout. Only minor uncertainty: final frame timing might be off by ±2 seconds due to frame-by-frame estimation, but penalty assessment is definitive.
cannot_determine: Exact completion timestamp to within 1 second - estimated from frame-by-frame analysis
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level peg transfer performance. Trainee demonstrated rapid, coordinated bilateral grasper control with flawless object placement. All 6 objects successfully transferred from left to right pegs, then reversed back to left pegs in 82 seconds with zero penalties. Performance clearly exceeds expert benchmark (40-60s typical expert time, but novice performers often require 60-120s and this represents strong intermediate-to-expert performance).
strengths: Rapid, confident instrument control and peg transfers
Perfect grasp security - no dropped objects throughout task
Smooth, efficient hand coordination between bilateral graspers
Flawless object placement on pegs with no misplacements
Clean reversal execution - seamless transition from forward to reverse transfer
Excellent time management - completed task well within expert benchmark
improvement_suggestions: Minor optimization: Could potentially reduce time by 5-10 seconds with slightly faster pickup/placement transitions, but current performance already demonstrates expert-level competency
Continue practicing to maintain consistency and explore sub-60-second completion times if aiming for top-tier performance
